title: TypeScript Migrations in Prisma Next
description: In Prisma Next, migrations are TypeScript files you can read, edit, and re-run with confidence. Every step is verified before it runs and again after, and every failure points directly at the operation that caused it.

title: MongoDB Without Compromise
description: Prisma Next brings a MongoDB-native experience to TypeScript with type-safe queries, real migrations, polymorphic models, embedded collections, and typed aggregation pipelines.

title: Announcing Query Insights For Prisma Postgres
description: Query Insights gives you built-in visibility into how your database queries behave in production. It is included in Prisma Postgres and works out of the box without any setup.

title: Agentic Engineering: How Prisma Builds with AI
description: In three months, Prisma fundamentally changed how it builds software by rethinking the relationship between engineers and AI agents. Here's what we learned building Prisma Next with agentic engineering.

title: Rethinking Database Migrations
description: Database migrations are brittle and they break when you're most vulnerable; when you're deploying to production. Prisma Next migrations make them explicit, verifiable and safe to retry.

title: Sunsetting Prisma Optimize
description: Prisma Optimize is being sunset as we move query observability directly into Prisma Postgres through Query Insights.

title: Prisma Next Roadmap
description: Have you been wondering when you can start using Prisma Next in your app? Take a look at our roadmap to find out.

title: Rebuilding the Prisma Docs: A New Framework, Design, and Structure
description: Learn how the Prisma team rebuilt their docs from scratch using Fumadocs, improved search with Mixedbread, and launched without disrupting day-to-day work.

title: The Next Evolution of Prisma ORM
description: A look at Prisma Next, a ground-up rethink of Prisma that improves queries, extensibility, migrations, and AI-friendly workflows while keeping Prisma’s schema-first approach.

title: Prisma ORM v7.4: Query Caching & Performance Boost
description: Prisma ORM v7.4 introduces query caching, partial indexes, and BigInt precision fixes—delivering significant performance improvements under load.

title: Prisma 7 Explained: Performance, Architecture, and AMA Answers
description: Prisma 7 introduces major architectural changes. We answer community AMA questions on performance, database support, the new client generator, and what’s next.

title: Prisma ORM 7.3.0: Faster Query Compilation, Safer BigInt JSON, Better Adapters
description: Prisma ORM 7.3.0 adds a new compilerBuild option (fast or small), speeds up raw queries by bypassing compilation, improves MSSQL and better-sqlite3 stability, reverts mapped enum behavior, and preserves BigInt precision in relationJoins JSON aggregat

title: Prisma ORM 7.2.0 Released: CLI Improvements, Bug Fixes & Better Configs
description: Prisma ORM 7.2.0 is out with key improvements: the return of the --url flag, smarter prisma init configs for Node and Bun, better CLI errors, and fixes for @db.Money. Upgrade today.

title: Understanding Prisma 7 Performance Benchmarks
description: We reviewed community benchmarks comparing Prisma 6 and Prisma 7. Here’s what they measure, where regressions exist, and how we’re fixing them.

title: Prisma 7 Release: Rust-Free, Faster, and More Compatible
description: Prisma ORM 7 delivers major performance gains, a Rust-free client, and a streamlined developer experience, plus deeper Prisma Postgres integration.

title: Announcing Prisma 6.19.0
description: Prisma 6.19 release includes pooled Postgres connections and VS Code extension improvements

title: How Deno and Prisma Partnered to Power Per-Branch Databases
description: Deno Deploy integrates Prisma Postgres for instant per-branch databases, enabling seamless full-stack deployment and modern developer workflows.

title: Prisma ORM 6.18.0, Postgres Database Metrics and Config Modernization
description: Catch up on the lates 6.18.0 release for Prisma ORM and Prisma Postgres.

title: The ORM Convergence: Convergence through experience
description: Why Drizzle's relational API v2 mirrors Prisma: production database problems at scale lead to similar patterns. A look at ORM convergence.

title: ORM v6.17.0, New Usage Metrics & Direct Connections in GA for Prisma Postgres
description: Prisma ORM v6.17.0 is out with fixes and improvements. Connecting to Prisma Postgres via your favorite ORM/DB tool is now GA. Read the full update.

title: Introducing Instant App Deployments in Partnership with Vercel
description: Instant App Deployment with Prisma Postgres and Vercel makes going from generated code to production effortless. Provision a Postgres database, deploy globally, and let users claim full ownership with a single API call. Perfect for AI coding platform
